actionable_feedback: Add an explicit input_citations column (or similarly named column) to crop_stress_index.csv that ties each row's decision to the exact packet source line (e.g., "./input/task.json:12 (flood_depth=1.1)").

Include a separate validation_checks_report.txt (or a clearly labeled 'validation' section in the audit) with runnable commands/snippets (shell or python) that reproduce the CSV from the input packet; keep the commands minimal and copy-pastable.
Ensure actual_outputs.artifact_refs (and top-level run metadata) lists all produced artifacts (audit.md and stdout) so graders/verifiers can locate evidence without relying solely on previews.
Update artifact preview metadata so the audit.md preview is not truncated (or attach the full audit as an additional artifact) to remove verifier uncertainty about cited lines/tables.
Keep the road-distance scoring formula and intermediate calculation steps in the audit, but mark the score as informational and show the exact numeric substitutions as already done.
feedback_summary: Baseline attempt correctly produced the required crop_stress_index.csv and a supporting audit; exclusion rules were applied consistently to the three cells and road-distance scores were computed and documented. The grader and verifier both awarded full scores. Two minor issues remain: (1) actual_outputs metadata lists only the CSV while the grader/verifier used audit.md and stdout as evidence - reconcile actual_outputs.artifact_refs to include those artifacts; (2) the audit.md preview is marked truncated in artifact previews, limiting direct verifier inspection of the final decision table. The grader recommended improving in-CSV citation clarity and adding a runnable validation snippet; the verifier recommended providing a full (untruncated) audit preview or fixing the metadata listing.
